entities.name: Serve
entities.description: The shot that initiates every rally in pickleball.

entities.name: Paddle
entities.description: The implement used to hit the ball in pickleball.

entities.name: Baseline
entities.description: The line at the back of the court where players start their serve.

entities.name: Service Court
entities.description: The area on the opponent's side of the net where serves are made.

entities.name: Power Serve
entities.description: A serve hit with maximum force aimed deep in the opponent's service court.

entities.name: Placement Serve
entities.description: A serve focused on accuracy rather than power, hitting the ball to a specific spot.

entities.name: Spin
entities.description: The rotation of the paddle or ball that affects its trajectory and bounce.

entities.name: Topspin
entities.description: Spin generated by brushing up on the ball, causing it to dip faster and bounce higher.

entities.name: Sidespin
entities.description: Spin generated by brushing across the ball, causing it to curve in the air.

entities.name: Backspin
entities.description: Spin generated by brushing down on the ball, causing it to float and bounce lower.

entities.name: Lob Serve
entities.description: A high, arcing shot that lands deep in the opponent's service court.

entities.name: return of serve
entities.description: The second shot in a pickleball rally, crucial for the receiving team to gain an advantage.

entities.name: two-bounce rule
entities.description: A regulation requiring the ball to bounce twice before being returned.

entities.name: deep return
entities.description: Hitting the ball deep into the opponent's court to push them back and set up a strong position for the receiving team.

entities.name: kitchen line
entities.description: The area near the net where players advance after hitting the return of serve.

entities.name: topspin return
entities.description: A return with topspin that dips faster and bounces higher, making it harder for the opponent to handle.

entities.name: slice return
entities.description: A return with slice that stays low and can force the opponent to hit up on the ball.

entities.name: cross-court return
entities.description: A return that creates a longer angle, potentially catching the opponent off guard.

entities.name: third shot
entities.description: A crucial shot in pickleball that separates beginners from intermediate and advanced players.

entities.name: third shot drop
entities.description: A soft, arcing shot that lands in the opponent's kitchen, forcing them to hit up on the ball.

entities.name: third shot drive
entities.description: A more aggressive option where the serving team hits the ball hard and low over the net.

entities.name: pickleball
entities.description: A sport played with a paddle and a small, lightweight plastic ball on a badminton court.

entities.name: Dinking
entities.description: A soft shot that lands in the opponent's kitchen, forcing them to hit up on the ball and creating opportunities to attack.

entities.name: Basic dink
entities.description: A gentle shot that just clears the net and lands in the kitchen.

entities.name: Cross-court dinking
entities.description: A variation where the ball is hit diagonally across the court, creating longer angles and giving the dinking team more time to react.

entities.name: Dink placement strategy
entities.description: Hitting the ball to the opponent's weaknesses based on their backhand or movement speed.

entities.name: Speed-up from the dink
entities.description: Suddenly hitting the ball harder after a series of soft dinks, catching the opponent off guard.

entities.name: Dink battles
entities.description: Long rallies where both teams try to create an opportunity to attack.

entities.name: Volleying
entities.description: Hitting the ball out of the air before it bounces.

entities.name: Punch Volley
entities.description: A short, punchy motion where the player pushes the ball with the paddle using a firm wrist and a short swing.

entities.name: Block Volley
entities.description: Used for hard shots that are hit directly at the player. The player puts the paddle in front of the ball and lets it bounce off the paddle.

entities.name: Volley Placement
entities.description: The strategic placement of the ball to force the opponent into weak shots or to regain position.

entities.name: Reset Volley
entities.description: A defensive shot used when under pressure, hitting a soft volley that lands in the opponent's kitchen.

entities.name: Erne
entities.description: An advanced shot in pickleball that involves jumping around the kitchen to hit a volley from outside the kitchen area.

entities.name: pickleball
entities.description: A sport played with a small ball and paddles on a badminton court.

entities.name: Overhead
entities.description: A powerful shot hit above the head in pickleball.

entities.name: Smash
entities.description: An aggressive shot used to put away high balls or lobs.

entities.name: Paddle
entities.description: The implement used in pickleball for hitting the ball.

entities.name: Net
entities.description: The vertical barrier that separates the two sides of a pickleball court.

entities.name: Opponent
entities.description: Another player in the game of pickleball.

entities.name: High ball
entities.description: A shot hit high into the air.

entities.name: Deep
entities.description: Far from the net.

entities.name: Footwork
entities.description: The movement of a player on the court to reach shots or defend against them.

entities.name: lob
entities.description: A high, arcing shot that goes over the opponent's head and lands deep in the court.

entities.name: offensive lob
entities.description: Hit with topspin to dip and bounce high; effective when the opponent is at the net and not expecting a lob.

entities.name: defensive lob
entities.description: Hit with backspin or slice to float and stay in the air longer; effective when under pressure and needing time.

entities.name: counter-lob strategy
entities.description: Anticipating the lob and moving back to hit it, involving overhead or groundstroke shots.

entities.name: Stacking
entities.description: A doubles formation where both players stand on the same side of the court.

entities.name: Forehand
entities.description: The player's dominant hand used for hitting the ball.

entities.name: Backhand
entities.description: The player's non-dominant hand used for hitting the ball.

entities.name: Left-hand right-hand stacking
entities.description: A specific stacking formation involving a left-handed player and a right-handed player standing on the same side.
